blocks:
Sūrat al-Ṭūr

Meccan, its number of verses is forty-nine verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالطُّورِ ** (By the Mount) It is said that He means Ṭūr Sīnīn (طور سنين), which is a mountain in Madyan where Moses (peace be upon him) heard the speech of Allah, and al-Qummiyy narrated what is close to this.

(٢) وَكِتابٍ مَسْطُورٍ ** (and a written Book) written (مكتوب).

(٣) فِي رَقٍّ مَنْشُورٍ ** (in an unrolled parchment) Al-Raqq (الرقّ) is the hide upon which writing is done; it is borrowed here for that upon which the Book is written. The indefinite form (تنكيرهما) of both is for magnification (التعظيم) and to indicate that they are not among what is commonly known among people.

(٤) وَالْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ ** (and the Frequented House) Al-Qummiyy said: It is in the fourth heaven, and it is al-Ḍirāḥ (الضراح). Seventy thousand angels enter it every day, and then they never return to it. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, Allah placed four pillars beneath the Throne (العرش) and named them al-Ḍirāḥ (الضراح), and it is الْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ ** (the Frequented House). He said to the angels: 'Circumambulate it (طوفوا به).' Then He sent angels and said: 'Build a house on Earth in its likeness and measure, and command those on Earth to circumambulate the House (البيت).'" And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: "Seventy thousand angels enter it every day, and then they never return to it." And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: الْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ ** (the Frequented House) is in the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The House (البيت) which is in the heaven is called al-Ḍurāḥ (الضُّراح), and it is directly above the Sacred House (البيت الحرام); if it were to fall, it would fall upon it. A thousand angels enter it every day, and then they never return to it.

I say: And in the narration of the Ascension (حديث المعراج), it is stated that it is in the seventh heaven. This was narrated by al-Qummiyy and al-'Ayyāshī.

(٥) وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ ** (5) By the raised ceiling, Al-Qummi said: the heaven (السماء). And he narrated it in al-Majma' from Ali (peace be upon him). (٦) وَالْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ ** (6) by the swollen sea. It was said: meaning the filled (المملوء), which is the encompassing ocean (المحيط), or the kindled (الموقد), derived from His saying: وَإِذَا الْبِحارُ سُجِّرَتْ ** (2) when the seas are set ablaze, And Al-Qummi said: It will be kindled on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And it was narrated: that Allah will make the seas fire on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), with which Hell (جهنّم) will be kindled.

(٧) إِنَّ عَذابَ رَبِّكَ لَواقِعٌ ** (7) Indeed the punishment of your Lord will occur. It is descending (لنازل). (٨) ما لَهُ مِنْ دافِعٍ ** (8) There is none who can avert it. Averts it (يدفعه). It was said that the aspect of the indication of these matters sworn by upon that (the punishment) is that they are matters which indicate the perfection of Allah's power and His wisdom, the truthfulness of His reports, and the recording of the servants' deeds for recompense. (٩) يَوْمَ تَمُورُ السَّماءُ مَوْراً ** (9) The day the heaven sways violently, It trembles (تضطرب). (١٠) وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبالُ سَيْراً ** (10) and the mountains move away swiftly. Al-Qummi (said): meaning, they move like the wind. And narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) in a hadith about the two blasts (النفختين), which was previously mentioned in Surat al-Zumar, He said: It means they are spread out (تبسط). (١١) فَوَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (11) So woe on that day to the deniers, (١٢) الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي خَوْضٍ يَلْعَبُونَ ** (12) those who play around in [vain] talk. Al-Qummi said: They plunge into acts of disobedience (المعاصي). (١٣) يَوْمَ يُدَعُّونَ إِلى نارِ جَهَنَّمَ دَعًّا ** (13) The day they will be violently pushed toward the Fire of hell: They are pushed toward it violently (بعنف). (١٤) هذِهِ النَّارُ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ بِها تُكَذِّبُونَ ** (14) 'This is the Fire which you used to deny. Meaning, that will be said to them. (١٥) أَفَسِحْرٌ هذا ** (15) Is this magic, or are you unable to see? Meaning, you used to say regarding the revelation (للوحي): "This is magic." So, is this realization (المصداق) also magic? أَمْ أَنْتُمْ لا تُبْصِرُونَ ** this, just as you were unable to see in the world that which indicates it? And this is a reproach (تقريع) and mockery (تهكّم). (١٦) اصْلَوْها فَاصْبِرُوا أَوْ لا تَصْبِرُوا ** (16) Enter it! So be patient or do not be patient, it is all the same to you. You are only being requited for what you used to do.' Meaning, enter it in whatever manner you wish, whether with patience or without it, for there is no escape (لا محيص) for you from it. سَواءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** meaning, the two matters are equal: patience and the lack thereof. إِنَّما تُجْزَوْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** This is the justification (تعليل) for the equality (للاستواء).

(١٧) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَنَعِيمٍ ** (17) The Godwary will be amid gardens and bliss, In which gardens and which bliss. (١٨) فاكِهِينَ ** (18) rejoicing in what their Lord has given them, and their Lord will save them from the punishment of the Hellfire. Enjoying comfort, delighting in بِما آتاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ وَوَقاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ عَذابَ الْجَحِيمِ (what their Lord has given them, and their Lord will save them from the punishment of the Hellfire). (١٩) كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا هَنِيئاً بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. (19) ‘Eat and drink to your heart’s content, for what you used to do.’ (٢٠) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلى سُرُرٍ مَصْفُوفَةٍ ** (20) reclining on couches arrayed [in rows], Lined up [in rows] (مصطفّة). وَزَوَّجْناهُمْ بِحُورٍ عِينٍ ** and We will wed them to wide-eyed houris. Their description has preceded in Surat al-Dukhan (سورة الدخان). (٢١) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاتَّبَعَتْهُمْ ذُرِّيَّتُهُمْ بِإِيمانٍ أَلْحَقْنا بِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ ** (21) We will unite with the faithful those of their descendants who follow them in faith, and We will not stint them of their deeds in any way. Every person is a hostage to what he has earned. And it was read (وقرئ) وَاتْبَعْناهُمْ وَذُرِّياتِهِمْ** (and We made their descendants follow them).

It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Indeed, Allah raises the descendants of the believer to his rank, even if they are below him, so that his eye may be comforted by them." Then He recited this Ayah.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, He said: "The children fell short of the deeds of their fathers, so the children were joined with the fathers so that their eyes might be comforted thereby."

In al-Majma', narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said: "The children of the believers will be presented to their fathers on the Day of Resurrection." And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated the like of it.

In al-Faqih, narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, entrusted Ibrahim (إبراهيم) and Sarah (سارة) with the children of the believers. They nourish them with a tree in Paradise that has udders (أخلاف) like the udders of cows, [residing] in a palace made of pearl (درّة). When the Day of Resurrection comes, they will be clothed, perfumed, and presented to their fathers. They will be kings in Paradise alongside their fathers. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاتَّبَعَتْهُمْ ذُرِّيَّتُهُمْ ** — the Ayah [continues].

** وَما أَلَتْناهُمْ ** and We will not stint them And We will not diminish them (وما نقصناهم). And it was read (وقرئ) with a kasrah on the lām (بكسر اللّام), which has the same meaning. **مِنْ عَمَلِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** of their deeds in any way. by this joining; rather, We bestow favor upon them.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (Those who have faith) refers to the Prophet and the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and their descendants (ذرّيته) are the Imams (الأئمّة) and the Successors (الأوصياء) عليهم السلام. أَلْحَقْنا بِهِمْ (We will unite with them), and We did not diminish their descendants of the proof (الحجّة) that Muhammad brought concerning Ali, and their proof is one, and their obedience is one.

كُلُّ امْرِئٍ بِما كَسَبَ رَهِينٌ ** Every person is a hostage to what he has earned. He is held hostage by his deed (بعمله مرهون) with Allah; if he performs a righteous deed, He releases him, and if not, He destroys him.

(٢٢) وَأَمْدَدْناهُمْ بِفاكِهَةٍ وَلَحْمٍ مِمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ ** (22) and We will supply them with fruit and meat, such as they desire. And We increased them, time after time, with what they desire of various kinds of blessings (النعم). (٢٣) يَتَنازَعُونَ فِيها ** (23) They will pass around in it They will exchange among themselves and their companions, pulling back and forth (بتجاذب), كَأْساً ** (a cup) of wine (خمراً). He named it by the name of its container (محلّها), and for this reason, He made its pronoun feminine (انّث ضميرها). لا لَغْوٌ فِيها وَلا تَأْثِيمٌ ** a cup wherein there is no vain talk, nor any sin. Meaning: They do not speak with vain talk (لغو الحديث) during the course of drinking it, nor do they commit what causes the doer to incur sin (يؤثم به فاعله), as is the custom of drinkers in this world (الدنيا). And that is similar to His saying: لا فِيها غَوْلٌ ** (wherein there is no intoxication) And it was read (وقرئ) with fathah (بالفتح). Al-Qummi said: There is no singing (غناء) or obscenity (فحش) in Paradise, and the believer drinks and does not incur sin (يأثم).

(٢٤) وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (24) And there will go around them meaning, with the cup, youths belonging to them, meaning, slaves specifically designated for them. And it was said that they are their children who preceded them. كَأَنَّهُمْ لُؤْلُؤٌ مَكْنُونٌ as if they were hidden pearls,** preserved in the shell due to their whiteness and purity.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: that He was asked: "The servant is like the pearl, so how is the served one?" So He said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, verily the superiority of the served one over the servant is like the superiority of the moon on the night of the full moon over the rest of the stars."

(٢٥) وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ يَتَساءَلُونَ ** (25) And some of them will advance toward others, questioning one another. They ask one another about their states and their deeds. (٢٦) قالُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبْلُ فِي أَهْلِنا مُشْفِقِينَ ** (26) They will say, 'Indeed, formerly, when we were among our folks, we were fearful [of Allah's punishment].' Al-Qummi said: meaning, fearful (خائفين) of the punishment. (٢٧) فَمَنَّ اللهُ عَلَيْنا ** (27) So Allah favoured us and saved us from the punishment of the scorching wind. [He favoured us] with mercy, and He protected us from the punishment of al-samūm (السَّمُومِ), which is the punishment of the Fire that penetrates the pores (المسام) just as poisons (السموم) penetrate. Al-Qummi said: al-samūm (السموم) is the intense heat (الحرّ الشديد). (٢٨) إِنَّا كُنَّا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (28) Indeed, formerly we used to supplicate Him. Indeed, He is the All-benign, the All-merciful.' [meaning] before that, in the world (الدنيا), نَدْعُوهُ ** (we worship Him). إِنَّهُ ** (and it was recited with fath (بالفتح)) هُوَ الْبَرُّ ** (the Benefactor (المحسن)) الرَّحِيمُ ** (the One possessing abundant mercy (الكثير الرحمة).) (٢٩) فَذَكِّرْ ** (29) So admonish. By the grace of your Lord, you are neither a soothsayer nor a madman. So persist in admonishing and do not pay attention to their saying. فَما أَنْتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ ** (by the praise of Allah and His favor) بِكاهِنٍ وَلا مَجْنُونٍ ** (as they say.) (٣٠) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شاعِرٌ نَتَرَبَّصُ بِهِ رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ ** (30) Do they say, 'A poet! We await for him the vicissitudes of fortune'? [Meaning] that which disturbs the souls from the accidents of time (حوادث الدّهر). And it is said that al-manūn (المنون) is death (الموت). (٣١) قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا فَإِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ ** (31) Say, 'Wait! I too am waiting along with you.' I await your destruction just as you await my destruction. (٣٢) أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلامُهُمْ ** (32) Do their intellects bid them to [say] this, or are they a rebellious lot? [meaning] their intellects (عقولهم). Al-Qummi said: There was no one in the world (الدنيا) more forbearing (احلم) than Quraysh. بِهذا ** (with this contradiction (التناقض) in speech, for the soothsayer (الكاهن) possesses shrewdness (فطنة) and keen insight (دقة نظر), while the madman (المجنون) has his intellect covered, and the poet (الشاعر) possesses imaginative, measured speech (كلام مخيّل موزون), and that cannot be achieved by the madman.) أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طاغُونَ ** (transgressing the limit in obstinacy (العناد).)

(٣٣) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُ ** (Do they say, 'He has fabricated it'?) He fabricated it from his own accord. بَلْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (Rather, they do not have faith.) So they cast these aspersions due to their disbelief and obstinacy. (٣٤) فَلْيَأْتُوا بِحَدِيثٍ مِثْلِهِ ** (Let them bring a discourse like it,) Like the Quran. إِنْ كانُوا صادِقِينَ ** (if they are truthful.) In their claim, since among them are many who are counted among the eloquent ones (الفصحاء). So, this is a refutation of the mentioned sayings by way of challenge (التحدّي), or specifically a refutation of the fabrication (التقوّل), because the rest of the categories are manifest in obstinacy (العناد).

(٣٥) أَمْ خُلِقُوا مِنْ غَيْرِ شَيْءٍ Or were they brought into existence, having come into being without a Creator (محدّث) and an Apportioner (مقدّر)? Therefore, they do not worship Him. أَمْ هُمُ الْخالِقُونَ Or did they create themselves? (35) Were they created from nothing, or are they the creators? (٣٦) أَمْ خَلَقُوا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بَلْ لا يُوقِنُونَ Since if they had certainty, they would not have turned away from His worship. (36) Did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they have no certainty. (٣٧) أَمْ عِنْدَهُمْ خَزائِنُ رَبِّكَ The treasuries of His provision, so that they may grant prophethood (النبوّة) to whomever they wish, or the treasuries of His knowledge, so that they may choose for it whomever they wish. أَمْ هُمُ الْمُصَيْطِرُونَ The dominators over things, managing them however they wish. And it was read with the letter س (السين). (37) Do they possess the treasuries of your Lord, or are they the controllers?

(٣٨) ** أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ ** (38) Do they have a ladder a means of ascent (مُرْتَقَى) to the heaven ** يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ** ascending upon it to the speech of the angels and what is revealed to them of the knowledge of the Unseen (عِلْم الْغَيْب) so that they may know what is to be. فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُمْ let their listener bring بِسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (38) ... a manifest authority. with a clear proof (حُجَّة وَاضِحَة) that confirms his listening.

**(٣٩) أَمْ لَهُ الْبَناتُ وَلَكُمُ الْبَنُونَ ** (39) Do the daughters belong to Him, and the sons to you?

And this is what Quraysh said: that the angels are the daughters of Allah. Thus did al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrate it. And in it is a declaration of their foolishness and an indication that whoever holds this opinion is not counted among the rational ones (العقلاء), let alone that he should ascend with his soul to the realm of the dominion (عالم الملكوت) and thus look upon the unseen matters (الغيوب).

(٤٠) أَمْ تَسْئَلُهُمْ أَجْراً ** (40) Or do you ask them a reward upon conveying the Message (الرسالة). فَهُمْ مِنْ مَغْرَمٍ ** so that they are weighed down by debt from the obligation of a debt (غرم), burdened (مُثْقَلُونَ), carrying the heavy load (الثقل), and therefore they are averse to following you. (٤١) أَمْ عِنْدَهُمُ الْغَيْبُ ** (41) Or do they possess the Unseen which is the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ), in which the unseen matters (المغيبات) are recorded, فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ ** so that they write it down? from it. (٤٢) أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْداً ** (42) Or do they intend a stratagem? It was said that this is their stratagem (كيدهم) in the House of Assembly (دار النّدوة) against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هُمُ الْمَكِيدُونَ ** But the faithless are the ones who are strategized against. They are the ones whom the stratagem (الكيد) encompasses (يحيق بهم) or upon whom the evil consequence (وبال) of their stratagem returns. It was said that this is their killing on the Day of Badr.

(٤٣) أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلهٌ غَيْرُ اللهِ ** (43) Do they have a god other than Allah? Who helps them and guards them from His punishment. سُبْحانَ اللهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (43) Clear is Allah of what they associate [with Him]! [Clear is He] of their associating partners, or the partnership (شركة) of what they associate with Him. (٤٤) وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً ** (44) And if they see a fragment A piece of the sky falling, they would say, due to the extremity of their transgression (طغيانهم) and obstinacy (عنادهم): سَحابٌ مَرْكُومٌ (a massed cloud). This is a cloud, some parts of which have accumulated upon others. And this is the answer to their saying: فَأَسْقِطْ عَلَيْنا كِسَفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ.** (37) So make fragments of the sky fall upon us.

(٤٥) فَذَرْهُمْ حَتَّى يُلاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي فِيهِ يُصْعَقُونَ ** (45) So leave them until they encounter their day when they will be thunderstruck. It was said that it is at the first blast (النفخة الأولى). (٤٦) يَوْمَ لا يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئاً ** (46) The day when their plotting will not avail them in any way, nor will they be helped. in repelling the punishment, وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ ** they will be prevented from the punishment of Allah. (٤٧) وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (47) Indeed, for those who do wrong there is a punishment besides that, Al-Qummi [said]: they wronged the family of Muhammad, the blessings of Allah be upon them, their right, عَذاباً دُونَ ذلِكَ ** meaning: besides the punishment of the Hereafter. Al-Qummi said: [it is] the punishment of the Return (الرجعة) by the sword. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** that.

(٤٨) وَاصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ ** (48) And be patient for the judgment of your Lord, (Be patient) with His granting them respite and your remaining in their distress (عنائهم). فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعْيُنِنا ** for indeed you are in Our eyes, (Meaning) in Our preservation (حفظنا) and Our protection (حرزنا), such that We see you and guard you (نكلؤك). And the pluralization of the eye (العين) is due to the plural pronoun (الضمير) and for the sake of emphasis on the abundance of the means of preservation (أسباب الحفظ). وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ ** and glorify your Lord with praise when you rise, Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: (This refers) to the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل).

(٤٩) وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ ** (49) And [some part] of the night, too, glorify Him He said: The night prayer (صلاة اللّيل). وَإِدْبارَ النُّجُومِ ** and at the retreat of the stars. And when the stars retreat (أدبرت) at the end of the night. And it was recited with fathah (on the alif of إدبار) meaning: following them (في أعقابها) when they set (اغربت) or disappear (خفيت).

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) regarding this Ayah, they said: Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would rise during the night three times, look at the horizons of the sky, and recite the five [Ayahs] from Āl 'Imrān, the last of which is: إِنَّكَ لا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعادَ ** You do not break the promise. Then He would commence the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل). (The narration continues).

And narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): وَإِدْبارَ النُّجُومِ ** means the two rak'ahs before the Fajr prayer (صلاة الفجر).

And it was narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and from 'Ali and Al-Hasan ibn 'Ali (peace be upon them). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Baqir, and in al-Qummi, narrated from Al-Rida (peace be upon them): the like of it.

And in Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Sūrat al-Ṭūr, Allah the Almighty will gather for him the good of this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخرة), if Allah the Almighty wills.
